content.full_text:
JERRY LEVANDER
6520 Selma Ave, #1294
Los Angeles, CA 90028
(213) 871-0114 • (213) 871-0466

FPPC

AUG 24 1992
RECEIVED

August 19, 1992


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA 95814

Attn: Bianca Breeze, Attorney at Law

Dear Ms. Breeze:

In this letter, I seek an urgent review on whether there would be a conflict of interest in my providing any or all of the compensated-for services described below, for the Yerba Buena Water Company, a public utility water corporation.

In 1987 that utility filed its Application No. 87-08-048 with the California Public Utilities Commission seeking authority to transfer ownership of the utility and for a certificate of public convenience and necessity to extent its service area.

As an Administrative Law Judge for the Public Utilities Commission, I was assigned to process that application. My duties included the drafting of a final order which was signed by the Commission as Decision No. 88-03-052. That decision authorized the ownership transfer. The requested certificate authority was denied without prejudice. The utility had not timely provided a required proponents environmental assessment.

In April 1990 the utility filed its new Application No. 90-04-010 seeking a certificate of public convenience and necessity to extend service to a portion of the previously requested service area. That application was amended on May 5, 1992.

I retired from state service on March 1, 1990. However, the Public Utilities Commission engaged me as an Administrative Law Judge on a consulting basis terminating on June 30, 1990. I did not work on processing A90-04-010 for the Commission.

After filing amended application A90-04-010, the utility's president contacted me to determine if I could assist the utility in processing their application.

SCO + S'O

[illegible]

Fair Political Practices Commission
August 19, 1992
Page 2


Before agreeing to assist the utility, I sought clearance from a Public Utilities Commission
Conflict of Interest Advisor. She in turn referred me to your office.

PROPOSED SCOPE OF EMPLOYMENT

1.	Consult with and advise applicant on its proposal.

2.	Represent applicant in discussions with the Public Utilities Commission staff to:

	a)	Ascertain any areas of dispute between staff and applicant;

	b)	Determine the nature of any further information requested by staff;

	c)	Cause the preparation of such information.

3.	If staff objections were resolved, request staff to inform the assigned Administrative Law
Judge that staff does not object to the granting of the application (on an ex parte basis
if no other matters require hearing).

4.	If the assigned Administrative Law Judge determined that a hearing on October 27, 1992
is necessary, represent and/or testify for applicant of the hearing.

I would appreciate an early telephone call confirmed by a written reply to permit my timely
preparation for the upcoming hearing or to let the utility obtain another consultant.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Jerry J. Levander

JJL:owp

sections.question: In this letter, I seek an urgent review on whether there would be a conflict of interest in my providing any or all of the compensated-for services described below, for the Yerba Buena Water Company, a public utility water corporation.
sections.question_synthetic: As a former PUC Administrative Law Judge who processed a 1987-1988 application for Yerba Buena Water Company and later served as a consulting ALJ through June 1990, would it constitute a conflict of interest to now represent the utility in processing its amended 1992 application before the PUC?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No conclusion section exists as this is the initial request seeking advice. Document contains detailed background on applicant's prior work and proposed scope of employment. Request marked as urgent due to upcoming October 27, 1992 hearing deadline.

embedding.summary: Jerry Levander, a retired state Administrative Law Judge, requests FPPC guidance on whether he can ethically represent Yerba Buena Water Company in processing a PUC application, given his prior involvement with the utility's 1987 application and subsequent consulting work for the PUC.
